steps.title: Identify the equations as lines and extract their slopes
steps.content:
The equations $y = x + 21$ and $y = 12x$ are in slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$, where $m$ is the slope. Each represents a line in the plane:
  • $y = x + 21$ has slope 1
  • $y = 12x$ has slope 12

steps.title: Apply the parallel lines test
steps.content: Two distinct lines either are parallel (zero intersections) or intersect at exactly one point. Parallel lines have equal slopes. Since the slopes are different ($1 \neq 12$), these lines are not parallel. Therefore, they intersect at exactly one point. That matches choice B — B is correct.
